operating_state: Total revenue increased to $4.2 billion for nine months ended September 30, 2022, driven by rental revenue growth and Real Estate Operations NOI rising $181 million to $2.5 billion from positive rental rate growth and development completions. Consolidated operating portfolio occupancy remained strong at 97.8% with rent change on rollover of 47.1%, reflecting customer demand outpacing supply across target markets. Operating cash flows increased to $2.8 billion from $2.3 billion driven by real estate operations segment growth and strategic capital revenues.
cost_structure: Rental expenses increased while maintaining strong NOI margins, with general and administrative expenses rising primarily due to equity-based compensation increases. Interest payments decreased net of capitalized amounts, while the company capitalized development-related G&A expenses. Depreciation and amortization remained stable at $1.2 billion for both periods.
material_events: Completed Duke Realty acquisition on October 3, 2022 for approximately $23 billion through equity issuance and debt assumption, adding 142 million square feet across 480 properties. Generated $2.1 billion in net proceeds from property contributions and dispositions realizing $977 million in net gains, while earning promote revenues primarily from PELF in Europe during third quarter. Issued $2.7 billion in senior notes including green project financing, terminated existing facility while establishing new $3.0 billion facility, and appointed Daniel Letter as President and Eugene Reilly as Vice Chairman effective January 1, 2023.
macro_exposures: Company monitors ongoing inflation, rising interest rates, and war and energy crisis in Europe pressuring global economy, with foreign currency exposure mitigated through natural hedging via borrowing in functional currencies and derivative instruments. Strengthening U.S. dollar generated net gains on settlement of undesignated derivatives offsetting negative translation impacts, while interest rate exposure exists through variable rate debt and refinancing activities.
forward_outlook: Management expects prudent capital deployment with focus on build-to-suit developments and pause on contributions to open-ended funds given uncertain macro environment, while believing company is well-positioned for organic revenue growth given market rent increases and high lease mark-to-market of approximately 62%. Combined company pro forma AFFO per share projected to reach $6.21 by 2026 with dividend per share growing over forecast period.
uncertainty_profile: Company faces risks from international economic and political conditions, changes in global financial markets and interest rates, and competition for properties. Environmental uncertainties include natural disaster risks and ongoing pandemic impacts, while venture partners retain option to cancel equity commitments starting 18 months after initial commitment date. Maintenance of REIT status and regulatory considerations present ongoing compliance requirements, while financing availability and credit ratings affect capital access.
